      • spyScoutsOwnedWithId(state: GameState; houseId: HouseId): tuple[id: string, scout: SpyScout]
Source   Edit  

Batch Iteration Patterns for Data-Oriented Processing

Provides iterators for common game state access patterns, enabling cache-friendly batch processing during turn resolution.

Design Philosophy:

  • Eliminate 99 repeated loop patterns across 22 files
  • Clear intent (coloniesOwned vs manual filtering)
  • Consistent semantics (always yields same entity type)
  • Batch-friendly (process all entities of type together)

Usage: for colony in state.coloniesOwned(houseId): # Process colony

NOTE: These are read-only iterators. For mutations, use state_helpers.

Design Notes:

Data-Oriented Benefits:

  • Batch processing: Process all entities of type together (cache-friendly)
  • Clear intent: coloniesOwned() vs manual if colony.owner == houseId
  • Consistent patterns: Always same iterator style across codebase
  • Type safety: Compiler ensures correct entity type usage

Read-Only Philosophy: These iterators are read-only to:

  1. Separate data access from mutation (clear separation of concerns)
  2. Force explicit mutations via state_helpers templates
  3. Enable parallel processing (read-only is parallelizable)
  4. Prevent accidental mutations via reference escaping

WithId Variants: Iterators with WithId suffix return tuples with IDs for cases where:

  • You need to mutate the entity (use with state_helpers)
  • You need the entity's identifier for logging/events
  • You're building a seq of (id, entity) pairs

Performance:

  • Iterators have zero allocation overhead (compiler optimizes to loops)
  • No closures created (inline expansion)
  • Same performance as hand-written loops
  • Better cache locality than scattered access patterns

Historical Context: 99 repeated loop patterns were found across 22 files. These iterators eliminate that duplication while improving readability.

Imports

gamestate, fleet, order_types, ../common/types/core

Iterators

iterator activeHouses(state: GameState): House {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Iterate non-eliminated houses

Example: for house in state.activeHouses(): # Process active house

Source   Edit   
iterator activeHousesWithId(state: GameState): tuple[id: HouseId, house: House] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Iterate active houses with IDs (for mutations)

Example: for (houseId, house) in state.activeHousesWithId(): state.withHouse(houseId): house.prestige += bonus

Source   Edit   
iterator allColoniesWithId(state: GameState): tuple[id: SystemId, colony: Colony] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Iterate all colonies with IDs (for batch processing)

Example: for (systemId, colony) in state.allColoniesWithId(): state.withColony(systemId): colony.production = calculateProduction(colony)

Source   Edit   
iterator allFleetsWithId(state: GameState): tuple[id: FleetId, fleet: Fleet] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Iterate all fleets with IDs (for batch processing)

Example: for (fleetId, fleet) in state.allFleetsWithId(): state.withFleet(fleetId): fleet.fuelRemaining -= 1

Source   Edit   
iterator allHousesWithId(state: GameState): tuple[id: HouseId, house: House] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Iterate all houses with IDs (for batch processing)

Example: for (houseId, house) in state.allHousesWithId(): state.withHouse(houseId): house.turnsWithoutOrders += 1

Source   Edit   
iterator blockadedColonies(state: GameState): Colony {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Iterate all currently blockaded colonies

Example: var blockadeCount = 0 for colony in state.blockadedColonies(): blockadeCount += 1

Source   Edit   
iterator blockadedColoniesWithId(state: GameState): tuple[id: SystemId,
    colony: Colony] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Iterate blockaded colonies with IDs (for mutations) Source   Edit   
iterator coloniesOwned(state: GameState; houseId: HouseId): Colony {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Iterate all colonies owned by a house

Example: var totalProduction = 0 for colony in state.coloniesOwned(houseId): totalProduction += colony.production

Source   Edit   
iterator coloniesOwnedWithId(state: GameState; houseId: HouseId): tuple[
    id: SystemId, colony: Colony] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Iterate colonies with system IDs (for mutations)

Example: for (systemId, colony) in state.coloniesOwnedWithId(houseId): state.withColony(systemId): colony.production = calculateProduction(colony)

Source   Edit   
iterator eliminatedHouses(state: GameState): House {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Iterate eliminated houses

Example: for house in state.eliminatedHouses(): echo house.name, " has been eliminated"

Source   Edit   
iterator fleetsAtSystem(state: GameState; systemId: SystemId): Fleet {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Iterate all fleets at a system

Example: var fleetsPresent: seqFleet = @ for fleet in state.fleetsAtSystem(systemId): fleetsPresent.add(fleet)

Source   Edit   
iterator fleetsAtSystemForHouse(state: GameState; systemId: SystemId;
                                houseId: HouseId): Fleet {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Iterate fleets of a specific house at a system

Example: var myFleetsHere: seqFleet = @ for fleet in state.fleetsAtSystemForHouse(systemId, myHouseId): myFleetsHere.add(fleet)

Source   Edit   
iterator fleetsAtSystemForHouseWithId(state: GameState; systemId: SystemId;
                                      houseId: HouseId): tuple[id: FleetId,
    fleet: Fleet] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Iterate house fleets at system with IDs (for mutations) Source   Edit   
iterator fleetsAtSystemWithId(state: GameState; systemId: SystemId): tuple[
    id: FleetId, fleet: Fleet] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Iterate fleets at system with IDs (for mutations)

Example: for (fleetId, fleet) in state.fleetsAtSystemWithId(systemId): if shouldRetreat(fleet): state.withFleet(fleetId): fleet.retreating = true

Source   Edit   
iterator fleetsOwned(state: GameState; houseId: HouseId): Fleet {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Iterate all fleets owned by a house

Example: var totalMaintenance = 0 for fleet in state.fleetsOwned(houseId): totalMaintenance += fleet.maintenanceCost

Source   Edit   
iterator fleetsOwnedWithId(state: GameState; houseId: HouseId): tuple[
    id: FleetId, fleet: Fleet] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Iterate fleets with IDs (for mutations)

Example: for (fleetId, fleet) in state.fleetsOwnedWithId(houseId): state.withFleet(fleetId): fleet.status = FleetStatus.Reserve

Source   Edit   
iterator fleetsWithOrders(state: GameState): tuple[id: FleetId, fleet: Fleet,
    order: FleetOrder] {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Iterate fleets that have persistent orders

Example: for (fleetId, fleet, order) in state.fleetsWithOrders(): # Execute fleet order

Source   Edit   
iterator fleetsWithStandingOrders(state: GameState): tuple[id: FleetId,
    fleet: Fleet, order: StandingOrder] {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Iterate fleets that have standing orders

Example: for (fleetId, fleet, standingOrder) in state.fleetsWithStandingOrders(): # Execute standing order

Source   Edit   
iterator spyScoutsAtSystem(state: GameState; systemId: SystemId): SpyScout {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Iterate spy scouts at a system

Example: for scout in state.spyScoutsAtSystem(systemId): # Process scout missions

Source   Edit   
iterator spyScoutsAtSystemWithId(state: GameState; systemId: SystemId): tuple[
    id: string, scout: SpyScout] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Iterate spy scouts at system with IDs (for mutations) Source   Edit   
iterator spyScoutsOwned(state: GameState; houseId: HouseId): SpyScout {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Iterate spy scouts owned by a house

Example: var activeScouts = 0 for scout in state.spyScoutsOwned(houseId): if not scout.detected: activeScouts += 1

Source   Edit   
iterator spyScoutsOwnedWithId(state: GameState; houseId: HouseId): tuple[
    id: string, scout: SpyScout] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Iterate spy scouts with IDs (for mutations) Source   Edit
